BNS.section_title: Cheating and Dishonestly Inducing Delivery of Property
BNS.description: Whoever cheats and thereby dishonestly induces the person deceived to deliver any property to any person, or to make, alter or destroy the whole or any part of a valuable security, or anything which is signed or sealed, and which is capable of being converted into a valuable security.
BNS.punishment: Imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, and shall also be liable to fine.
evidence_required: Proof of false representation or deceptive promise made by the accused.
Evidence of delivery of property or money (bank receipts, screenshots, cash receipts).
Proof of dishonest intention (mens rea) existing at the time of transaction (emails, chat logs, call recordings).

BNS.description: Whoever, intending to take dishonestly any movable property out of the possession of any person without that person's consent, moves that property in order to such taking, is said to commit theft.
BNS.punishment: Imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years, or with fine, or with both, and in the case of a second or subsequent conviction, with rigorous imprisonment which may extend to five years and with fine.
evidence_required: Proof that the property was movable and was in possession of the complainant.
Proof that it was taken without consent (CCTV footage, eye-witness testimonies).
Dishonest intention to take the property out of possession.

BNS.description: Whoever, being in any manner entrusted with property, or with any dominion over property, dishonestly misappropriates or converts to his own use that property, or dishonestly uses or disposes of that property in violation of any direction of law prescribing the mode in which such trust is to be discharged.
BNS.punishment: Imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to five years, or with fine, or with both.
evidence_required: Proof of entrustment of property or dominion over property to the accused.
Evidence of dishonest misappropriation, conversion, or disposal of the property by the accused (ledger logs, bank accounts, sale receipts).
Violation of contract or express direction of trust.

BNS.description: Whoever, by words either spoken or intended to be read, or by signs or by visible representations, makes or publishes any imputation concerning any person intending to harm, or knowing or having reason to believe that such imputation will harm, the reputation of such person.
BNS.punishment: Simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both, or with community service.
evidence_required: Proof of publication of defamatory statements (screenshots of social media, news clippings, recordings).
Proof that the statement refers specifically to the complainant.
Proof of harm to reputation or malicious intent to defame.

BNS.description: Whoever enters into or upon property in the possession of another with intent to commit an offence or to intimidate, insult or annoy any person in possession of such property, or having lawfully entered, unlawfully remains there.
BNS.punishment: Imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three months, or with fine which may extend to five thousand rupees, or with both.
evidence_required: Proof of complainant's exclusive possession of the property (land records, lease deed).
Unauthorized entry or remaining unlawfully by the accused (photographs, eye-witnesses).
Intent to commit an offense, intimidate, insult, or annoy.

BNS.description: Whoever does any act with the intention of thereby causing hurt to any person, or with the knowledge that he is likely thereby to cause hurt to any person, and does thereby cause hurt to any person, is said voluntarily to cause hurt.
BNS.punishment: Imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine which may extend to ten thousand rupees, or with both.
evidence_required: Medical examination report confirming bodily pain, disease, or infirmity.
Proof of intentional action or knowledge of potential harm (eyewitness testimony, physical proof).

BNS.description: Voluntarily causing hurt which results in permanent privation of sight, hearing, member, joint, destruction of powers, permanent disfiguration of head/face, bone fracture, or bodily pain for a period of fifteen days.
BNS.punishment: Imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, and shall also be liable to fine.
evidence_required: Detailed medical officer certificate or X-ray proving fracture or severe injury.
Proof of weapon used or gravity of assault.

BNS.description: Whoever makes any false document or false electronic record or part of a document or electronic record, with intent to cause damage or injury to the public or to any person, or to support any claim or title, or to cause any person to part with property.
BNS.punishment: Imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.
evidence_required: Forged physical document or altered digital file.
Forensic report or proof of signature fabrication.
Intent to defraud or cause injury.

BNS.description: When two or more persons agree to do, or cause to be done: (a) an illegal act, or (b) an act which is not illegal by illegal means, such an agreement is designated a criminal conspiracy.
BNS.punishment: If the conspiracy is to commit a serious offense, same punishment as abetment of such offense; otherwise imprisonment up to six months, or fine, or both.
evidence_required: Proof of agreement between two or more parties (call logs, shared messages, joint bank transactions).
An overt act committed by one or more parties in pursuance of the conspiracy agreement.

BNS.description: Whoever threatens another with any injury to his person, reputation or property, or to the person or reputation of any one in whom that person is interested, with intent to cause alarm to that person, or to cause that person to do any act which he is not legally bound to do.
BNS.punishment: Imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.
evidence_required: Proof of threatening communication (audio files, SMS text, WhatsApp screenshots, call recordings).
Proof that the threat was intended to cause alarm or force actions.

BNS.description: A person is said to cheat by personation if he cheats by pretending to be some other person, or by knowingly substituting one person for another, or representing that he or any other person is a person other than he or such other person really is.
BNS.punishment: Imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to five years, or with fine, or with both.
evidence_required: Evidence of false identity assumed by the accused (fake profiles, forged identity cards).
Deceptive communication inducing complainant.

BNS.description: A person is guilty of a public nuisance who does any act or is guilty of an illegal omission which causes any common injury, danger, annoyance or obstruction to the public or to the people in general who dwell or occupy property in the vicinity.
BNS.punishment: Fine which may extend to five thousand rupees.
evidence_required: Proof of act causing obstruction or annoyance (photographs, neighborhood statements).
Impact on public space or local residents.

BNSS.section_title: Information in Cognizable Cases (First Information Report)
BNSS.description: Every information relating to the commission of a cognizable offence, if given orally to an officer in charge of a police station, shall be reduced to writing by him or under his direction. This section also permits electronic communications (e-FIR) which must be signed by the informant within three days.
BNSS.procedural_route: Filing an FIR (First Information Report) at the local Police Station or online (e-FIR). The police must register it, provide a free copy to the informant, and initiate an investigation. If police refuse to register, the informant can send the grievance to the Superintendent of Police (SP) under Section 173(4).

BNSS.description: A Magistrate having jurisdiction may take cognisance of an offence on receiving a complaint of facts which constitute such offence, and examine the complainant and the witnesses present under oath.
BNSS.procedural_route: Filing a Private Criminal Complaint directly in the Court of the Judicial Magistrate. The Magistrate examines the complainant under oath, records statement, and if satisfied of a prima facie case, issues summons/warrants to the accused under Section 227 BNSS.

BNSS.section_title: Power of Magistrate to Try Summarily
BNSS.description: Any Chief Judicial Magistrate or Metropolitan Magistrate may, if he thinks fit, try in a summary way all or any of the petty offences like theft (property value under Rs. 20,000), criminal trespass, insult, and simple hurts.
BNSS.procedural_route: Summary Trial procedure, designed for swift judicial disposal. Trials are conducted rapidly, and the maximum sentence of imprisonment awarded cannot exceed three months.

BSA.description: Evidence may be given in any suit or proceeding of the existence or non-existence of every fact in issue and of such other facts as are hereinafter declared to be relevant, and of no others.
BSA.evidence_rule: Establishes what facts can be presented in court. Fact must directly support the claim (e.g. transfer of money in cheating) or dispute the opponent's assertion.

BSA.description: Any information contained in an electronic record which is printed on a paper, stored, recorded or copied in optical or magnetic media produced by a computer shall be deemed to be also a document and shall be admissible in any proceedings, without further proof or production of the original, if the conditions mentioned in this section are satisfied (including a mandatory Certificate to prove device integrity).
BSA.evidence_rule: Requires a signed Electronic Evidence Certificate (similar to old 65B IEA certificate) for screenshots, PDFs, emails, or call recordings to be legally admissible. The certificate must confirm that the device was operating properly when the record was captured.

BSA.description: The burden of proof as to any particular fact lies on that person who wishes the Court to believe in its existence, unless it is provided by any law that the proof of that fact shall lie on any particular person.
BSA.evidence_rule: The complainant holds the initial burden to establish their claim with admissible proof.
